seoTitle: MacroSilicon Products | USB Video Capture, HDMI Converters | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Explore MacroSilicon's comprehensive product portfolio including USB video capture chips, HDMI/MIPI interface converters, video scalers, and multimedia SoC solutions.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by MacroSilicon?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon offers four core product categories: USB Video Capture Chips for streaming and recording, HDMI/MIPI Interface Converters for display connectivity, Video Scaler and Processor ICs for resolution conversion, and Multimedia SoC Platforms for integrated solutions. Each category includes multiple product series optimized for different applications and markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right MacroSilicon components for your video processing needs.

faqs.question: How do I select the right USB video capture solution?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right MacroSilicon USB capture chip involves analyzing your input source (HDMI, analog, or camera), required resolution (1080p or 4K), frame rate needs (30fps or 60fps), and target USB interface (USB 2.0 or USB 3.0). For HDMI 1080p60 capture, MS2130 is ideal. For 4K capture, choose MS2131. For analog sources, MS2109 provides universal compatibility. Consider output format requirements (MJPEG, YUY2) and software compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify input source and resolution first, then match with appropriate capture chip. Contact FAE for complex requirements.

faqs.question: What support does BeiLuo provide for MacroSilicon products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive support including product selection guidance for video applications, reference design review and customization, firmware and driver support, evaluation kit provision, supply chain management, competitive pricing, and fast delivery. Our FAE team has deep expertise in video processing and interface conversion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo FAE team for design assistance and product recommendations.

faqs.question: Which MacroSilicon products support 4K resolution?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon offers several 4K-capable products. The MS2131 USB capture chip supports HDMI 2.0 input up to 4K30. The MS9332 HDMI to MIPI DSI converter supports 4K display output. The MS1820 video scaler can process 4K resolutions for up/down conversion. For 4K streaming applications, the MS2131 provides hardware encoding to reduce host CPU load. Check individual datasheets for specific 4K format and frame rate support.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 4K capture, use MS2131. For 4K display conversion, use MS9332. Verify format compatibility with your specific requirements.

faqs.question: How do MacroSilicon HDMI converters handle HDCP?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon HDMI converters have varying HDCP support depending on the model and application. USB capture chips like MS2130/MS2131 typically do not support HDCP-encrypted content capture for compliance reasons. HDMI to display converters like MS9332 pass through HDCP signals transparently. For applications requiring HDCP handling, contact MacroSilicon or BeiLuo to discuss specific requirements and available solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For non-protected content, standard chips work. For HDCP content, verify specific model capabilities or contact FAE.

categories.name: USB Video Capture Chips
categories.description: High-performance USB video capture solutions for HDMI, analog, and camera interfaces, enabling streaming, recording, and video processing applications.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for MacroSilicon's USB video capture chip portfolio. These chips enable high-quality video capture from various sources including HDMI, analog (CVBS/S-Video), and camera interfaces. With USB 3.0 and USB 2.0 options, support for resolutions up to 4K, and UVC compliance for plug-and-play operation, MacroSilicon capture chips are ideal for streaming, recording, video conferencing, and industrial vision applications. The portfolio includes cost-effective 1080p solutions and high-performance 4K capture options.

selectionGuide.title: USB Video Capture Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to choose the right USB capture chip for your application

faqs.question: What is the difference between MS2130 and MS2131?
faqs.answer: MS2130 supports HDMI input up to 1080p60 with USB 3.0 output, ideal for HD streaming and capture. MS2131 supports HDMI 2.0 input up to 4K30 with USB 3.0, designed for 4K capture applications. Both are UVC-compliant and support MJPEG/YUY2 formats. MS2131 includes hardware scaling for resolution conversion. Choose MS2130 for cost-sensitive 1080p applications, MS2131 for 4K requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MS2130 for 1080p applications. Use MS2131 for 4K capture needs.

faqs.question: Do MacroSilicon capture chips work with OBS Studio?
faqs.answer: Yes, all MacroSilicon USB capture chips are UVC (USB Video Class) compliant, making them compatible with OBS Studio, XSplit, Zoom, Teams, and other popular software without requiring proprietary drivers. They appear as standard webcam devices in Windows, Mac, and Linux. Recommended settings: Use MJPEG format for best performance, YUY2 for lowest latency. 1080p60 requires USB 3.0 port.
faqs.decisionGuide: All MacroSilicon capture chips work with OBS. Ensure USB 3.0 for 1080p60.

faqs.question: What input sources are supported?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon capture chips support various input sources: MS2130/MS2131 support HDMI 1.4/2.0 input from cameras, gaming consoles, set-top boxes, and PCs. MS2109 supports analog CVBS and S-Video from legacy devices, VCRs, and cameras. All chips support standard video resolutions from 480i to 4K depending on model. Audio is embedded in HDMI or available as separate analog input on some models.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose HDMI models (MS2130/MS2131) for digital sources. Choose MS2109 for analog sources.

faqs.question: What is the latency of USB capture?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon USB capture chips offer low latency suitable for live applications. Typical latency: 50-80ms end-to-end for 1080p60 on USB 3.0. Latency varies based on resolution, USB interface, and host processing. Hardware encoding in MJPEG reduces host CPU load. For lowest latency, use YUY2 format and ensure adequate USB bandwidth. Gaming and streaming applications typically find this latency acceptable.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use USB 3.0 and YUY2 format for lowest latency. Expect 50-80ms typical latency.

faqs.question: Can I capture from gaming consoles?
faqs.answer: Yes, MacroSilicon capture chips work with PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch, and other gaming consoles via HDMI output. MS2130 captures up to 1080p60 for current-gen consoles. MS2131 supports 4K30 for high-resolution capture. Note: HDCP-protected content cannot be captured due to copyright restrictions. Disable HDCP on console if available, or use non-protected output modes.
faqs.decisionGuide: MS2130 for 1080p gaming capture. MS2131 for 4K gaming. HDCP content restricted.

products.name: USB 3.0 HDMI Video Capture Chip
products.shortDescription: High-performance USB 3.0 HDMI capture chip supporting 1080p60 input with MJPEG/YUY2 output for streaming applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MS2130 is a high-performance USB 3.0 video capture chip that converts HDMI input to USB Video Class (UVC) output.
Supporting HDMI 1.4 input up to 1080p60 resolution, it delivers high-quality video capture for streaming, recording, and conferencing applications.
With hardware MJPEG encoding and uncompressed YUY2 support, the MS2130 minimizes host CPU usage while maintaining excellent video quality.
features: USB 3.0 UVC compliant - plug and play

faeReview.summary: The MS2130 is our most popular USB capture chip, offering an excellent balance of performance, cost, and ease of use. In my experience supporting streaming and recording applications, this chip consistently delivers reliable 1080p60 capture with minimal CPU overhead.
pros: Low latency suitable for live applications
faeReview.recommendation: Highly recommended for 1080p streaming, game capture, and video conferencing applications. Use USB 3.0 for best performance. Contact FAE for driver customization needs.

comparison.use_case: Choose MS2131 for 4K, MS2130 for 1080p cost savings
Use MS2109 for analog sources, MS2130 for HDMI

companionParts.relationship: HDMI to MIPI converter for display applications
companionParts.notes: Use with MS2130 for dual HDMI input systems
Pre-process video before capture if needed

faqs.question: What is the maximum capture resolution?
faqs.answer: MS2130 supports HDMI input up to 1920x1080 (1080p) at 60 frames per second. It also supports common lower resolutions: 720p60, 1080i60, 1080p30, and various PC resolutions. The chip automatically detects input resolution and adjusts accordingly. Output resolution matches input or can be scaled in software.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 1080p60 capture, MS2130 is ideal. For 4K, upgrade to MS2131.

faqs.question: Does it require special drivers?
faqs.answer: No, MS2130 is UVC (USB Video Class) compliant and works with standard drivers built into Windows, Mac OS, and Linux. It appears as a standard webcam device. No proprietary drivers needed for basic operation. Custom drivers available from MacroSilicon for special requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plug and play on all major OS. No driver installation required.

faqs.question: What software is compatible?
faqs.answer: MS2130 works with any UVC-compatible software including: OBS Studio, XSplit, vMix (streaming), Zoom, Teams, Skype (conferencing), VLC, AMCap (recording), Adobe Premiere, Final Cut (editing). Also compatible with DirectShow, V4L2, and AVFoundation frameworks for custom application development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compatible with all major streaming and conferencing software.

faqs.question: Can it capture audio?
faqs.answer: Yes, MS2130 captures HDMI embedded audio and outputs it through USB as standard PCM audio stream. Supports 2-channel stereo at 48kHz sample rate. Audio is synchronized with video. No separate audio connection needed when using HDMI input with embedded audio.
faqs.decisionGuide: Audio captured automatically from HDMI. No additional wiring needed.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption?
faqs.answer: MS2130 consumes approximately 400-500mW during operation, well within USB 3.0 power budget (900mA @ 5V = 4.5W). Can be bus-powered from USB port without external power supply. Power consumption varies slightly based on resolution and encoding format.
faqs.decisionGuide: USB bus powered. No external power supply required.

products.name: USB 3.0 4K HDMI Video Capture Chip
products.shortDescription: Advanced USB 3.0 capture chip with HDMI 2.0 input supporting 4K30 resolution for professional streaming and recording.
descriptionParagraphs: The MS2131 is MacroSilicon's flagship USB capture chip, supporting HDMI 2.0 input up to 4K (3840x2160) at 30fps.
With hardware scaling and MJPEG encoding, it delivers high-quality 4K capture while maintaining compatibility with USB 3.0 bandwidth constraints.
The chip supports downscaling to 1080p for streaming while simultaneously capturing 4K, making it ideal for professional content creation.

faeReview.summary: The MS2131 is the go-to solution for 4K capture applications. I've supported numerous professional streaming setups using this chip, and the hardware scaling feature is particularly valuable for simultaneous 4K recording and 1080p streaming.
pros: Excellent video quality with HDR support
faeReview.recommendation: Recommended for professional 4K streaming, content creation, and high-resolution recording. The hardware scaling feature is a key advantage for dual-output applications.

comparison.use_case: Use MS2130 for 1080p, MS2131 for 4K requirements

faqs.question: Does MS2131 support 4K60?
faqs.answer: No, MS2131 supports 4K (3840x2160) at up to 30fps. For 4K60 applications, consider using a different solution or contact MacroSilicon for roadmap information. The chip can capture 1080p at 60fps, and 4K30 is the maximum for 4K resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum 4K30. For 4K60, contact FAE for alternative solutions.

faqs.question: Can it stream 1080p while recording 4K?
faqs.answer: Yes, MS2131 features hardware scaling that can simultaneously output 4K for recording and 1080p for streaming. This is done through hardware scaler before USB output, reducing host CPU load. Both streams maintain high quality. Perfect for content creators who want 4K archives with 1080p live streams.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use hardware scaling for dual-output applications. Reduces CPU load significantly.

categories.name: HDMI/MIPI Interface Converters
categories.description: High-performance interface converters bridging HDMI, MIPI DSI/CSI, LVDS, and TTL interfaces for display and camera applications.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for MacroSilicon's interface converter portfolio. These chips enable seamless connectivity between different video interfaces, converting HDMI to MIPI DSI for smartphone displays, MIPI CSI to HDMI for camera modules, and various other interface combinations. With support for resolutions up to 4K, multiple lane configurations, and advanced features like HDR and color space conversion, these converters are essential for display modules, camera systems, and multimedia devices.

selectionGuide.title: Interface Converter Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to choose the right interface converter for your display or camera application

faqs.question: What is the difference between MIPI DSI and MIPI CSI?
faqs.answer: MIPI DSI (Display Serial Interface) is for connecting to displays - it carries video data from host to display panel. MIPI CSI (Camera Serial Interface) is for connecting to cameras - it carries image data from camera sensor to host processor. They use similar physical layer but different protocols. MacroSilicon offers converters for both: MS9332 converts HDMI to MIPI DSI for displays, while MS9601 converts MIPI CSI to HDMI for cameras.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DSI for displays, CSI for cameras. Choose converter based on your source and target interfaces.

faqs.question: What resolutions are supported?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon interface converters support a wide range of resolutions. MS9332 supports up to 4K (3840x2160) at 60fps for high-resolution displays. MS9601 supports up to 1080p60 for camera applications. Lower resolutions like 720p, 480p are also supported. The chips automatically detect input resolution and configure output accordingly. Check specific datasheets for detailed resolution support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your display or camera resolution requirements. MS9332 for 4K displays, MS9601 for 1080p cameras.

products.name: HDMI to MIPI DSI Converter
products.shortDescription: High-performance HDMI 1.4 to 4-lane MIPI DSI converter supporting up to 1080p60 for smartphone and tablet displays.
descriptionParagraphs: The MS9332 is a high-performance HDMI to MIPI DSI converter designed for smartphone, tablet, and portable display applications.
Supporting HDMI 1.4 input up to 1080p60 and 4-lane MIPI DSI output, it enables seamless connection between HDMI sources and MIPI display panels.
With integrated scaler, color space conversion, and advanced image processing, the MS9332 delivers excellent display quality.
features: RGB to RGB/YUV color space conversion

faeReview.summary: The MS9332 is an excellent solution for connecting HDMI sources to MIPI displays. I've used it in numerous smartphone display projects and it consistently delivers reliable performance with good image quality.
pros: Low power suitable for battery devices
faeReview.recommendation: Recommended for smartphone displays, portable monitors, and embedded display applications. Ensure proper MIPI trace routing for best results.

faqs.question: What MIPI DSI versions are supported?
faqs.answer: MS9332 supports MIPI DSI v1.01 and is compatible with most MIPI DSI display panels. It uses 4 data lanes plus clock lane. Data rate up to 1Gbps per lane. Supports video mode operation. Command mode not supported. Compatible with most smartphone and tablet display panels from major manufacturers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your panel's MIPI DSI version and lane configuration. MS9332 works with most video mode panels.

products.name: MIPI CSI to HDMI Converter
products.shortDescription: MIPI CSI-2 to HDMI converter for camera modules, supporting up to 1080p60 output for surveillance and imaging applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MS9601 converts MIPI CSI-2 camera signals to standard HDMI output for monitoring and recording.
Supporting 2/4-lane MIPI CSI-2 input up to 1080p60 and HDMI 1.4 output, it enables easy integration of MIPI cameras with HDMI displays.
With built-in ISP functions and flexible configuration, the MS9601 is ideal for surveillance, industrial vision, and medical imaging.

faeReview.summary: The MS9601 is perfect for bridging MIPI cameras to HDMI displays. The built-in ISP is a valuable feature that simplifies camera integration. I've used it successfully in surveillance and industrial vision projects.
faeReview.recommendation: Recommended for surveillance monitors, industrial cameras, and any application needing MIPI camera to HDMI display conversion.

companionParts.relationship: USB capture for recording MIPI camera output
companionParts.notes: Combine for MIPI camera to USB recording

faqs.question: What camera sensors are compatible?
faqs.answer: MS9601 works with any MIPI CSI-2 compliant camera sensor. Tested with Sony IMX series, OmniVision sensors, and Samsung sensors. Supports RAW8/10/12 and YUV422 input formats. Sensor initialization via I2C interface. Reference drivers available for popular sensors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your sensor's MIPI CSI-2 compatibility. Contact FAE for specific sensor support.

categories.name: Video Scaler and Processor ICs
categories.description: Advanced video processing chips for resolution conversion, image enhancement, and format conversion in professional AV and consumer applications.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for MacroSilicon's video scaler and processor portfolio. These chips perform high-quality resolution scaling, frame rate conversion, deinterlacing, and image enhancement. With support for multiple input/output formats and advanced processing algorithms, they are ideal for professional AV equipment, broadcast systems, and high-end consumer electronics.

selectionGuide.title: Video Scaler Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to choose the right video scaler for your processing needs

faqs.question: What scaling algorithms are used?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon video scalers use advanced polyphase scaling algorithms with multiple tap filters for high-quality up and down scaling. MS1820 uses 5-tap horizontal and 3-tap vertical filters. MS1850 uses enhanced 8-tap filters with adaptive edge enhancement. Both support various scaling ratios from 1:8 to 8:1. The algorithms preserve image detail and minimize artifacts during scaling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Both chips offer excellent quality. MS1850 for highest quality requirements, MS1820 for cost-sensitive applications.

products.name: High-Performance Video Scaler
products.shortDescription: Versatile video scaler supporting up to 1080p with advanced deinterlacing and image enhancement for AV applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MS1820 is a high-performance video scaler designed for professional AV and broadcast applications.
Supporting input resolutions up to 1080p and flexible output scaling, it handles format conversion between different video standards.
With integrated deinterlacing, color space conversion, and image enhancement, the MS1820 delivers broadcast-quality video processing.

faeReview.summary: The MS1820 is a versatile scaler that delivers excellent quality at a reasonable price. I've used it in broadcast and medical applications with great results. The deinterlacing quality is particularly good for this price range.
faeReview.recommendation: Recommended for professional AV, broadcast, and medical applications requiring high-quality 1080p scaling.

comparison.use_case: Use MS1850 for highest quality, MS1820 for cost-effective scaling

faqs.question: Does it support frame rate conversion?
faqs.answer: Yes, MS1820 supports frame rate conversion between common rates: 24fps, 25fps, 30fps, 50fps, 60fps. Uses motion-adaptive frame interpolation for smooth conversion. Also supports 3:2 pulldown detection and removal for film content. Frame rate conversion can be combined with scaling in a single pass.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable frame rate conversion as needed. Works well with scaling operations.

categories.name: Multimedia SoC Platforms
categories.description: Integrated multimedia SoC solutions combining video processing, encoding, and system control for smart displays and streaming devices.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized MacroSilicon distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for MacroSilicon's multimedia SoC portfolio. These highly integrated chips combine ARM processors, video codecs, graphics acceleration, and extensive I/O interfaces in a single device. They are ideal for smart displays, streaming boxes, video conferencing systems, and other multimedia applications requiring both processing power and video expertise.

selectionGuide.title: Multimedia SoC Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to choose the right SoC for your multimedia application

faqs.question: What operating systems are supported?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon SoCs support Linux and Android operating systems. Linux SDK includes kernel, drivers, and middleware. Android SDK includes full AOSP with customizations. Both include video codec libraries and hardware acceleration APIs. Reference designs include boot loaders and development tools. Contact MacroSilicon for SDK availability and licensing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Linux for embedded applications, Android for consumer devices with UI requirements.

products.name: 4K Multimedia SoC Platform
products.shortDescription: High-performance multimedia SoC with quad-core ARM Cortex-A53, 4K video codec, and rich interfaces for smart display applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MS8100 is a highly integrated multimedia SoC designed for smart displays, streaming devices, and video conferencing systems.
Featuring a quad-core ARM Cortex-A53 processor, 4K H.265/H.264 codec, and comprehensive I/O interfaces, it provides a complete solution for multimedia applications.
With built-in WiFi/BT connectivity, multiple display outputs, and camera interfaces, the MS8100 reduces BOM cost and accelerates time to market.

faeReview.summary: The MS8100 is a powerful SoC that combines processing power with excellent video capabilities. I've supported several smart display projects using this chip and the integration level significantly reduces design complexity.
faeReview.recommendation: Recommended for smart displays, streaming boxes, and any application needing both processing and 4K video. The integration level provides significant system cost savings.

faqs.question: What development tools are provided?
faqs.answer: MacroSilicon provides comprehensive development tools including: Linux/Android SDK with board support package, GCC toolchain and debugger, Hardware design reference including schematics and PCB layout, Evaluation board with debug interfaces, Technical documentation and application notes, FAE support for design and debugging. SDK includes video codec libraries and hardware acceleration APIs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request SDK and evaluation board early in design phase. Reference design accelerates development.
